mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or stays near the zero lower bound, the discount rate on future earnings is compressed and equity valuations re-rate upward. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month after easing continues.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing periods do not produce positive average 20-day SPX returns and the sign accuracy falls below 55% in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in CPIAUCSL above the prevailing trend raises inflation surprise risk, compresses real valuation multiples, and increases the odds of negative near-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.3pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-upshift months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time or if the average forward return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises while PAYEMS stagnates, labor-market deterioration signals weakening household income and future demand, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold episodes with weak payroll growth do not produce negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if PAYEMS growth alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in RSAFS indicates improving consumer spending and revenue momentum, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS trends fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns and the forward-return mean is not above the unconditional sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO falls while HOUST also weakens, simultaneous industrial and housing softness points to a broad cyclical slowdown that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in INDPRO and HOUST do not correspond to negative 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone captures the effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment regime with stable payroll growth supports domestic income and consumption, which should lift equity prices over the next week through better near-term risk appetite.
regime_hint: late-cycle but still expanding labor market
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment episodes with nonnegative payroll growth fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns or if the edge disappears once PAYEMS is included.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in GDPC1 signals improving aggregate output and earnings capacity, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter when the expansion is still intact.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP upturns are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than baseline or if the rank correlation is effectively zero over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a combination of higher FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL signals policy pressure and inflation risk, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above its 6-month average while CPIAUCSL inflation rate is above trend
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-inflation episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns or if the signal loses predictive power once either proxy is removed.
